dsm.interpretation: Clean layering: all dependencies flow downward
Clean layering: all dependencies flow downward

delta.ratchet_rationale: Three clusters removed (one 2-file pair from baseline level 1, one 2-file pair from baseline level 2). Two clusters re-emerged at adjacent depths after the import graph rebalanced, but neither corresponds to the cycles being fixed. Per AST module-level SCC analysis, only 2 SCCs remain (both inside #1336's blocks.registry <-> ai.agent.* tangle); sentrux's clustering algorithm reports 3 file-level clusters within that same SCC. The remaining 3 sentrux clusters are all attributed to #1336.

remaining_scc_attribution.members: 16 modules including blocks.registry, blocks.ai.ai_block, blocks.app.app_block, blocks.app.bridge, blocks.code.code_block, blocks.code.validation, blocks.io.materialisation, ai.agent.terminal, ai.agent.system_prompt, ai.agent.mcp, ai.agent.mcp._context, ai.agent.mcp.tools_authoring, ai.agent.mcp.tools_inspection, ai.agent.mcp.tools_qa, ai.agent.mcp.tools_workflow, workflow.validator

attribution_note: Manager-owned baseline + post-integration evidence for the no-cycles umbrella PR. Baseline at 5 sentrux clusters; predicted 5 -> 2 in plan but actual is 5 -> 3 due to sentrux's clustering algorithm reporting tighter subgroups within #1336's 16-module SCC. Python AST module-level SCC count: 4 -> 2 (cleaner number, matches the plan intent). The `max_cycles` ratchet uses sentrux's count as authoritative since `.sentrux/rules.toml: max_cycles` is checked against sentrux output, not AST SCC count. After #1336 closes, sentrux clusters drop to 0 and the no_cycles import-linter contract (tracked at #1341) can land. Tech-debt for the lazy import added by Track B is tracked at #1342.
